title: `podman --version` inside WSL2 VM does not match `podman --version` under windows (v4.4.4)
body:
### Issue Description

The current WSL image (`podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v36.0.144.tar`) is shipped with 4.4.1, thus `podman --version` inside WSL2 VM does not match `podman --version` under windows (v4.4.4)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install v4.4.4 under windows
2. Run `podman --version`
    ```
    podman.exe version 4.4.4
    ```
3. Run `podman machine init`
  ```
  $ podman --log-level=debug machine init
time="2023-03-30T13:38:25+02:00" level=info msg="C:\\Program Files\\RedHat\\Podman\\podman.exe filtering at log level debug"
Extracting compressed file
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
time="2023-03-30T13:38:35+02:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [--import podman-machine-default C:\\Users\\USER\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\wsldist\\podman-machine-default C:\\Users\\USER\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v36.0.144.tar --version 2]"
time="2023-03-30T13:38:37+02:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default rpm --restore shadow-utils]"
  ```
4. Start the machine with `podman machine start`
5. Run `podman machine ssh podman --version`
    ```
    podman version 4.4.1
    ```


### Describe the results you received

Podman version inside VM: 4.4.1

### Describe the results you expected

Podman version inside VM: 4.4.4

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman info
host:
  arch: amd64

  conmon:

  kernel: 5.4.72-microsoft-standard-WSL2

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +WASM:wasmedge +YAJL
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 10

### Additional information

n/a

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @boldt!

It takes a while until the packages inside the VMs/images are updated. @baude and @ashley-cui are working on improving that.

Is it more a cosmetic issue for you or did you run into issues?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have not heard back and this should have been updated, closing.
